sentence: Technology is transforming how we communicate and work.
notes: Vague narrative with no specific checkable assertion.

sentence: I believe renewable energy is the most important challenge of our time.
notes: First-person opinion. Should be skipped by the detector.

sentence: The following section examines recent trends in artificial intelligence.
notes: Transition sentence with no factual claim.

sentence: Many experts consider the current geopolitical situation highly uncertain.
notes: Hedged with 'many experts consider' and 'highly uncertain'. No specific claim.

sentence: It is widely argued that education is the foundation of economic opportunity.
notes: Hedged opinion framing. Not a specific verifiable assertion.

sentence: The company's journey from a small startup to a global brand is an inspiring story.
notes: Narrative and subjective. No measurable or checkable claim.

sentence: We will explore these themes further in the conclusion.
notes: Transition/framing sentence.

sentence: Critics have raised concerns about the pace of artificial intelligence development.
notes: Vague reference to unnamed critics with no specific claim.

sentence: The debate over data privacy remains unresolved across most jurisdictions.
notes: Editorial/narrative characterization of a broad situation.

sentence: Startups are frequently described as engines of innovation and job creation.
notes: Hedged descriptor. 'frequently described as' removes verifiability.

sentence: Progress on climate policy has been slower than many researchers had hoped.
notes: Comparative and hedged. No specific policy, metric, or date.

sentence: The last decade has been shaped by the rapid adoption of smartphones worldwide.
notes: Narrative framing. 'Shaped by' is not a specific checkable assertion.

sentence: Some argue that regulation in this sector has not kept pace with technological change.
notes: Hedged opinion attributed to unnamed group.

sentence: The United States has 50 states.
true_fact: The United States has 50 states.
notes: Baseline sanity check. Should be trivially supported.

sentence: The iPhone was first released in June 2007.
true_fact: The original iPhone was announced by Steve Jobs on January 9, 2007 and went on sale June 29, 2007.
notes: Draft: Apple iPhone launched June 29 2007. Verify date precision.

sentence: NASA was established in 1958.
true_fact: NASA was created by the National Aeronautics and Space Act, signed July 29, 1958.
notes: Draft: NASA Act signed July 1958. Verify year.

sentence: Wikipedia was launched in January 2001.
true_fact: Wikipedia was launched on January 15, 2001.
notes: Draft: Wikipedia launched January 15 2001. Verify.

sentence: Python was created by Guido van Rossum.
true_fact: Python was created by Guido van Rossum, with development beginning in the late 1980s.
notes: Draft: widely documented attribution. Verify.

sentence: Amazon was founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994.
true_fact: Amazon was incorporated by Jeff Bezos on July 5, 1994.
notes: Draft: Amazon incorporated 1994. Verify.

sentence: Microsoft was co-founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen.
true_fact: Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen on April 4, 1975.
notes: Draft: Gates and Allen founded Microsoft 1975. Verify co-founder attribution.

sentence: The Berlin Wall fell in November 1989.
true_fact: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
notes: Draft: November 9 1989. Verify.

sentence: Google was founded in 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin.
true_fact: Google was incorporated on September 4, 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin.
notes: Draft: incorporated September 4 1998. Verify year and founders.

sentence: SpaceX was founded by Elon Musk in 2002.
true_fact: SpaceX was founded by Elon Musk in May 2002.
notes: Draft: founded May 2002. Verify.

sentence: The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair in Paris.
true_fact: The Eiffel Tower was constructed as the entrance arch for the 1889 Exposition Universelle in Paris.
notes: Draft: built for the 1889 Exposition Universelle. Verify.

sentence: OpenAI was founded in 2015.
true_fact: OpenAI was announced in December 2015.
notes: Draft: announced December 2015. Verify exact year.

sentence: World War II ended in 1945.
true_fact: World War II ended in 1945, with Germany surrendering May 8 and Japan surrendering September 2.
notes: Baseline sanity check. Should be trivially supported.

sentence: The iPhone was first released in 2008.
true_fact: The iPhone was first released in June 2007, not 2008.
notes: Off by one year. The correct year is 2007. Tests whether the grader catches a subtle year error.

sentence: NASA was established in 1957.
true_fact: NASA was established in 1958, not 1957. 1957 is when Sputnik launched.
notes: Off by one year. 1957 is when Sputnik launched, which is a plausible confusion. Verify the correct year.

sentence: Wikipedia was launched in 2003.
true_fact: Wikipedia was launched in January 2001, not 2003.
notes: Off by two years. Verify 2001 is correct.

sentence: Microsoft was founded in 1980 by Bill Gates and Paul Allen.
true_fact: Microsoft was founded in 1975, not 1980. 1980 is when Microsoft moved to Bellevue, Washington.
notes: Off by five years. 1980 is a plausible confusion since it is when the IBM PC deal happened. Verify correct founding year.

sentence: Amazon was founded by Jeff Bezos in 1997.
true_fact: Amazon was founded in 1994, not 1997. 1997 is when Amazon went public.
notes: Off by three years. 1997 is Amazon's IPO year, a plausible confusion. Verify correct founding year.

sentence: The United States has 48 states.
true_fact: The United States has 50 states. It had 48 states before Alaska and Hawaii joined in 1959.
notes: 48 was the count before 1959. Verify the correct current total.

sentence: SpaceX was founded in 2004 by Elon Musk.
true_fact: SpaceX was founded in 2002, not 2004.
notes: Off by two years. Verify correct founding year.

sentence: The Berlin Wall fell in 1991.
true_fact: The Berlin Wall fell in November 1989, not 1991. 1991 is when the Soviet Union dissolved.
notes: Off by two years. 1991 is when the USSR dissolved, a plausible confusion. Verify 1989 is correct.

sentence: Google was founded in 1999 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin.
true_fact: Google was founded in 1998, not 1999.
notes: Off by one year. Verify correct founding year.

sentence: Tesla delivered approximately 1.2 million vehicles in 2023.
true_fact: Tesla delivered approximately 1.81 million vehicles in 2023, not 1.2 million.
notes: 1.2M is significantly below the actual figure. This also appears in sample.txt and both providers flagged it. Verify the correct 2023 delivery count.

sentence: Alphabet reported annual revenue of $250 billion in 2023.
true_fact: Alphabet reported annual revenue of approximately $307 billion in 2023, not $250 billion.
notes: $250B is materially wrong. Also appears in sample.txt. Verify correct 2023 revenue figure.

sentence: OpenAI was founded in 2016.
true_fact: OpenAI was announced in December 2015, not 2016.
notes: Off by one year. Verify whether the announcement vs incorporation date matters here.

sentence: Apple was founded in 1977 by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak.
true_fact: Apple was founded in 1976, not 1977. Ronald Wayne was also a co-founder.
notes: Off by one year and omits Ronald Wayne. Verify whether the grader catches the year vs the missing co-founder.

sentence: The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1900 World's Fair in Paris.
true_fact: The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair, not the 1900 World's Fair.
notes: Off by 11 years. Both 1889 and 1900 had World Fairs in Paris, making this a plausible-sounding wrong claim. Good stress test.
